methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $860M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.1B - $8.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Apex Space
private.rationale: Same sector (Space & Aerospace), same stage (Series D), post-money $2.3B

data.narrative:
Stoke Space, a Series D-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $860M to date, is evaluated against a comparable universe of 5 public companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers spanning similar stages and sectors.

Two valuation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy and applying a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement, implies a valuation of $1.3B. Peer-median triangulation across four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Sierra Space ($8.0B, Series C), Apex Space ($2.3B, Series D), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B, Series C), and Skyryse ($1.1B, Series C)—yields a median of $1.9B, with a range spanning $1.1B to $8.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied: while the Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 25.8x price-to-sales across 6 public tickers, Stoke Space's revenue is not disclosed, preventing revenue-multiple application.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a valuation range of $1.1B (low) to $2.2B (high), with a median of $1.6B. The wide peer dispersion—particularly Sierra Space's $8.0B outlier—introduces material uncertainty around sector multiples. Key variables affecting valuation include revenue trajectory, customer contract visibility, and stage-specific capital efficiency relative to peer cohorts.
